- Enable ruff C901 rule (max-complexity=10) in pyproject.toml to
  enforce cyclomatic complexity as stated in constitution

  refactor (handle_authorization, post_with_retry)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

Docs: amend constitution to v2.0.0 (principle restructure)

Restructure principles to match turnovercal constitution format:
- Expand Code Quality with docstring, type annotation, complexity reqs
- Split TDD into dedicated Test-Driven Development and Phased
  Development principles
- Merge Pre-Commit Integrity into Atomic Commits & Compliance
- Resolve all TODOs: fill UX rules with captive-portal specifics,
  add concrete performance baselines from spec
- Add captive-portal constraints (Argon2, HA add-on, audit logging)

Feature/collision resolution (#11)

- Implement integration tests for the organize command, covering various scenarios including file organization by type, dry-run mode, manifest generation, naming conventions, and error handling.
- Implement integration tests for the scan command, verifying functionality for scanning directories, nested structures, file type filtering, and error handling.

fix(scan): enhance config loading mechanism

fix(manifest): add backward compatibility for manifest structure

- Update the manifest generator to include an operations property as an alias for entries for backward compatibility.

fix(operation): prevent execution of operations when no operations are present

- Add a critical early return in the operation executor to avoid executing performOperation when there are no operations to perform.

commit.message:
SCRUM-79: Fix Commit Messages (#26)

* revamped rust module prototype

* Checkpoint 2 for commit msg refactor. Consider pipeline.md for restructure

* Phase 2 complete of plan.md

* refactor(desktop): migrate all ALS interpretation to Rust NAPI parser

- Add diff_from_snapshot() export: diffs committed snapshot JSON against
  current ALS file, skipping HEAD blob download on the fast path
- Add generateCommitMessage() export: ports buildCommitMessage() logic
  from JS to Rust, removing the last JS function that read DiffReport data
- Convert all NAPI exports to async via tokio::task::spawn_blocking,
  keeping the Electron main process unblocked during ALS parsing
- Add schema_version field to Project struct with serde default for
  backwards-compatible snapshot deserialization
- Rewrite project.ts from ~800 lines to 61: remove all fast-xml-parser-
  based XML parsing, decompression, structuralCompareAls, buildLocalDiffFromAls,
  getAlsContent, and related helpers; retain only gitBin + git shell commands
- Replace get-als-content and no-commits fallback handlers with parseAls
- Remove buildCommitMessage() from main.ts
- Delete 9 orphaned types from types/index.ts (DecompressedAls, AlsChange,
  StructuralCompareResult, AudioTrackEntry, MidiTrackEntry, etc.)
- Uninstall fast-xml-parser (removes 68 packages)

JS is now purely transport: paths and buffers only. All ALS semantics
live in the Rust crate. One parser to update if Ableton's format changes.

* chore(desktop): remove old dist/vendor parser, restore git binary

* Fix build and ordering issue by implementing sorting the hashmap

* Remove outdated PIPELINE Claude file.

* Update apps/desktop/native/semantic-diff/src/diff.rs

Fix issue of unexpected characters when overflowing integers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update "children" model for the als structure to use a default so it doesn't fail in the event of empty children

commit.message:
Fix security/performance audit issues #1540 #1541 #1544 #1546 #1547 #1548 (#1549)

Security fixes:
- Require authentication on all mutation endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH)
  for binary API, basket, checkout, vector, agent, action routes (#1540)
- Filter sensitive fields (PasswordHash, PasswordSalt, MfaSecret, etc.)
  from API responses in EntityToJson() and GetEntityField (#1541)
- Validate query field names against entity metadata before processing
  legacy single-field filter in BuildQueryDefinition (#1544)

Performance/constraint fixes:
- Replace GetType() in DataQueryEvaluator with static type resolution:
  schema ClrTypes for virtual fields, ordinal switch for base fields,
  pattern match for untyped fallback (#1547)
- Fix pre-existing Contains_ListCaseInsensitive bug by inferring
  collection element types from value when schema unavailable
- Determined ConcurrentDictionary.ContainsKey (lock-free read) and
  ConfigureAwait(false) (no SyncContext in ASP.NET Core) are
  non-issues; no changes needed (#1546)
- Rewrite bmw-guard.sh: exclude test projects and build output,
  target specific forbidden APIs (JsonSerializer.Serialize/Deserialize),
  allow startup-time reflection in DataScaffold/MetadataExtractor,
  remove false-positive patterns (typeof, interface I, etc.) (#1548)

commit.message:
fix: cross-platform path traversal detection and WSL test resilience

Security: replace filepath.ToSlash with strings.ReplaceAll for backslash
normalization in containsDotDot() and IsSensitivePath(). filepath.ToSlash
is a no-op on Linux (backslash is not os.PathSeparator), allowing
Windows-style traversal paths like ..\etc\passwd to bypass detection.

WSL: add timeout and WSL_SKIP escape hatch to mage testwsl target.
cmdOutputTimeout helper prevents hangs when WSL is unresponsive.

commit.message:
feat(collab): crdt-safe Y.Map element storage

- Replace Y.Array('elements') with Y.Map('elementsMap') + Y.Array('elementOrder')
  for keyed, CRDT-safe element storage (no duplicate-on-concurrent-delete/insert)
- Shallow-clone elements on Y.Map write ({ ...el }) to snapshot versionNonce
  at write time; prevents Excalidraw's in-place mutations from making stored
  references always match the latest state (causing 0 changes detected)
- Shallow-clone elements on Y.Map read for updateScene; prevents Excalidraw
  from holding Y.Map's stored objects and mutating them during move/resize,
  which was causing move operations to never emit changes to peers
- Observer origin guard: only fire updateScene for 'remote'/'init' transactions,
  not local ones, to prevent handleChange → observer → handleChange loops
- fingerprint (id:versionNonce) guard in handleChange to skip no-op onChange
  calls (e.g. appState-only changes) and prevent re-emitting remote state
- Server-side lazy migration: getOrCreate detects old Y.Array('elements')
  snapshots and migrates them to Y.Map/Y.Array on first load

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(stream): race condition in pub/sub + migration for missing index

## pub/sub race condition (slow message delivery after reconnect)

spawn_inbox_wakeup() runs in a background task and takes ~50 ms to
complete TCP-connect + SUBSCRIBE. If a message arrived during that
window, its PUBLISH was missed, and the next delivery was at the
fallback poll — previously 30 s.

Fixes:
1. Synthetic wakeup sent immediately after SUBSCRIBE completes.
   This triggers an extra poll_messages() that catches any messages
   dispatched during the subscribe race window. Also fires on every
   pub/sub reconnect (desirable — catches messages missed during drop).

2. Fallback poll interval: 30 s → 5 s.
   Caps worst-case delivery delay (concurrent reconnects, Redis blip)
   without flooding the shared queue mutex.

## migration 033: missing index on user_blocks(blocked_user_id)

Migration 019 created the user_blocks composite PK
(blocker_user_id, blocked_user_id). Only the left column is indexed.
Queries like 'who has blocked me?' (is_blocked_by in shared/db.rs)
filter by blocked_user_id and caused full sequential scans.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(messaging): batch Redis ops into single lock + add timing spans

dispatch_envelope (Kafka-disabled path) previously acquired the queue
mutex THREE times sequentially: dedup-check, stream-write, sender-store.
Between each lock/unlock tokio could yield to poll_messages, causing
unnecessary contention and context-switch overhead on every send.

Changes:
- Kafka-disabled path: single mutex acquisition for all 3 Redis ops
  (dedup mark → XADD+PUBLISH → store_sender), released once at the end
- Kafka fallback path: stream-write + sender-store combined in one lock
- send_message RPC: log dispatch_ms after dispatch_envelope returns
- dispatch_envelope: log wait_ms (time to acquire lock) + redis_ms (batch)
- poll_messages: log lock_wait_ms + xread_ms when either exceeds 20 ms
- fix(sentinel): clippy dead_code, needless_question_mark, unnecessary_cast

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
